name: Equipment Carrier
description: Soft skinned Carrying Device similar to a loadbearing backpack. Halves all weight of the items inside when the Pack is properly worn on the Character’s back. Carries 18 Units.

name: Equipment Pouch
description: A partially armored carrying case with an Armor Rating of 8. Able to carry 5 Magazines, Grenades, and Clips. Once the Hardened Equipment Pouch takes 15 Damage, it no longer offers an Armor Rating. This does not stack with the Character’s Armor.

name: Gravitational Holster
description: A small, localized gravitational field that can be used to hold a weapon to the Character’s Armor in a desired location. Will move the weapon to the Character’s Hand when attempting to reach for it, giving the Character the effects of the Quickdraw Ability when used.

name: Extended Deployment Pack
description: A large tactical military backpack for extended travel and long-term deployment. Halves all weight of the items inside when the Pack is properly worn on the Character’s back. Designed to be quickly released off of the back in case of Combat. -10 Agility when worn. Carries 32 Units.

name: Ammunition Pouch
description: An ammunition pouch that can carry 1 Plasma Battery or 6 Magazine, Grenades, and Clips.

name: Personal Pouch
description: A common bag for carrying personal equipment. Carries 12 Units.

name: Thigh Rig
description: An armored carrying device that connects to the Armor on a Character’s Thigh. The Rig has 13 Armor but does not stack with the Character’s Armor. Able to carry 3 Magazines, Grenades, and Clips, or sidearm.

name: Antigravity Grappler
description: A gravity-powered set of pincer arms that can carry its own weight and up to 1,500 Kilograms. A Character must be focused to using the Tool and it can only work with both hands. When used against a Character, the Antigravity Grappler adds +60 Strength to any Opposed Strength Tests. Gives a +30 Bonus to Grappling Tests against a single Target. If the User has their Arms targeted or is forced to let go of the Antigravity Grappler, it will instantly turn off and drop anything it was holding.

name: Weapon Holder
description: A stationary weapon holster device that sits on the ground or affixes to a wall. Allows a Character to store a single Weapon or Equipment in a small antigravity field. Can hold up to 40 kg. Commonly used by Covenant forces as a way to show and brag of their prized weapons out of combat.

name: Gravity Carrier Rig
description: Armor mountable gravity weapons carrier. Can hold 2 weapons or 10 magazines, grenades, and clips. Allies can take weapons or items from the carrier as if they had the quickdraw ability.

name: Hardened Equipment Pouch
description: An armored carrying device with an Armor Rating of 15. Able to carry 5 Magazines, Grenades, and Clips. Once the Hardened Equipment Pouch takes 15 Damage, it no longer offers an Armor Rating. This does not stack with the Character’s Armor.

name: Standard Holster
description: A strong leather-like bond that holds the weapon to the Character.

name: Tactical Deployment Pack
description: A large tactical military backpack for travel and extended missions. Halves all weight of the items inside when the Pack is properly worn on the Character’s back. -5 Agility when worn. Designed to be quickly released off of the back in case of Combat. Carries 26 Units.

name: Medical Rucksack
description: A Medical extension to the M/LBE Hard Case. Can hold up to two Medical Kits, 10 Biofoam Canisters, and 10 sets of medication of the Player’s choice.

name: Pouch
description: Ammunition pouch. Carries 3 Units. Must be mounted to a Utility Webbing and Magnetic Webbing.

name: Softcase
description: Soft bagged Carrying Device similar to a duffel bag. Carries 13 Units.

name: Hard Case
description: A briefcase-like armored carrying device. Capable of carrying up to 14 Units. The Hard Case has 50 Breakpoints and an Armor Rating of 12 that does not stack with the Armor on the Character if struck.

name: Hard Case Pouch
description: Armored ammunition pouch. Carries 3 Units. Has an Armor Rating of 12 that does not stack with the Armor on the Character if struck. Must be mounted to a Utility Webbing and Magnetic Webbing.

name: M/LBE Hard Case
description: Blast-Proof backpack. 16 Armor. Carries 12 Units.

name: Small/Assault Pack
description: A small personal military backpack. Halves all weight of the items inside when the Pack is properly worn on the Character’s back. Carries 10 Units.

name: Medium Patrol Pack
description: A medium personal military backpack.  Halves all weight of the items inside when the Pack is properly worn on the Character’s back. Carries 14 Units.

name: Large/Heavy Pack
description: A standard large military backpack. Halves all weight of the items inside when the Pack is properly worn on the Character’s back. Carries 20 Units.

name: Tactical Hard Case
description: A pouch-like armored carrying device. The Tactical Hard Case has an Armor Rating of 11. Capable of carrying up to 6 Units. Must be mounted to a Utility Webbing or Magnetic Webbing.

name: Tactical Softcase
description: Soft bagged carrying pouch. Carries 7 Units. Must be mounted to a Utility Webbing and Magnetic Webbing.

name: Backpanel Pack
description: A military backpack. Designed for others to quickly access behind the User. Carries 8 Units.
